title: Oedera longipes
intro: Oedera longipes is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a shrubby daisy or a succulent herb. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Oedera longipes include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oedera longipes is often prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in topical creams and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Oedera longipes is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is further divided into various subfamilies and tribes, with Oedera longipes being a part of the tribe Gnaphalieae. The tribe Gnaphalieae is primarily composed of shrub-like plants, and Oedera longipes is no exception. Oedera longipes is also classified as a genus within the subfamily Gnaphalioideae. Further classification of Oedera longipes includes its species classification within the plant kingdom.
